## [00:00 - 03:04]

So the first thing I did was I said, I'm going to write a book. So I went and I wrote a book. This is a book I... Earlier I mentioned I took three years to write this thing. I'm actually a very gifted writer. And if I say so myself, so I wrote a really good, very readable book. This wasn't just some lousy, thrown together thing that I had some ghosts ready to do. I struggled over this thing. I wrote this excellent, excellent book. And tons of charts. There must have been a hundred different charts in there that were annotated. And it was just awesome. Even specialty trading books sold through a specialty trading catalog or whatever. $50 is a lot of money. So a book, let's just call it a trading book anywhere from $20 to $50. And I mean, this was... I put an enormous amount of work into this. And I had probably 5,000 people on my list. Then another 5,000, maybe 6,000 on my list of prospects. So I didn't have a really huge universe to sell this to. So if I was going to sell it for even $50, it wasn't going to make much money.

## [03:04 - 06:06]

I was going to sell a couple hundred of them at $50. That's $10,000. Just not my idea of a good time. I needed to get a higher price. And the reality was, this was amazing material in this product. Just freaking amazing. I mean, this is sort of a lifetime of secrets. It was... you know, my partner was a 20, 25 years of experience. I had 10 years of experience. It was like 30 plus years of a trading experience that I had spent years distilling down. And it was incredible, incredible stuff. And it was really well written. It was very clear and gave a clear plan and direction. It wasn't just a bunch of stuff thrown out there. So I was like, okay, this is not acceptable to sell for this kind of price. It's like a lifetime of experience in the financial markets. So this is what I did. I took this book. This is the book. And I split it into two books. So right there, now you're going to get two books and two books are worth what? Twice what? Twice what? One book is worth instead of $20 to $50. We'll call it $40 to $100, right? So I split them logically. This was like... And I don't know... I can't remember exactly what I called them. But basically it came down to theory or the what. And then the how. So this was like all the background they needed that gave them why this stuff worked and how to figure this stuff out. And here was to actually how to trade it in the market. So I split it and the 220 pages probably ended up getting blown out to, I don't know, 240 or 250 pages. And because some of it was overlap and stuff like that. And so we ended up... I think it probably ended up being 160 pages and 80 pages. So I added a few pages. Some of the pages were overlapped. You've got the old title page and all that stuff and the intros. Two different intros and two different conclusions. So it had probably been about 240 pages and now I had two books. Okay. So that was the first step. Then I'm like, okay, two books is a good start, but I got to blow this out some other way. So then what I did was... Video. And I fired up my screen capture program. Back in those days it was Camtasia. And I shot two hours of video of basically going through charts and applying these techniques and showed people how I applied these techniques. So I ended up with two hours of video.

## [06:06 - 09:08]

And I put it on two separate CDs. It was back then. I didn't do DVDs. I did two different CDs, an hour of video on each. So now I had two books and two CDs. Okay. I'm like, okay, now we're getting somewhere. Okay. Then I added in... Audio. And what I did was I had myself and my partner get on the phone and we talked... This is from memory now. I think it was probably... Let's just call it two hours. And it was two CDs. Okay. So now we had two audio CDs, two video CDs and two books. Same basic amount of information, or not the same amount of information, but the same basic information that I went over. And so here was... This was just simply a discussion between the two of us. I had a bunch of questions and I asked my partner those questions and then I expounded on his answers. So all of a sudden we had two more high quality hours right there. Then what we did was I found three experts. And these are three experts that were actually familiar with our material and followed our material. And I interviewed each one of them. So that added in... And each interview ran about an hour. So that added in three more CDs. Okay. So now we're up to three plus two plus two is seven CDs. You know, two or video, five of them are audio CDs. Okay. Then I added in a quick start. And this was simply me talking for, I don't know, let's say it was 20 to 30 minutes. And I just recorded myself basically telling people how to get started, how they should start because I was not giving them all this material.

## [09:08 - 12:08]

So I gave them, you know, basically, this is the thing. If you're going to be a teacher, if you're going to help people out, part of it is you're not just being a teacher, but you're being a coach. So this was like basically get them fired up, tell them how to start, where they should start, depending on who they were and what they were bringing to the table. Should they start with the book? Should they start with the theory? The how to? Should they start with the audio? Tell them what they're going to get out of each of these pieces. And so that was another CD. There's one more CD. It was an audio CD. And I added in a written quick start, which basically told them what was in the package, how to work with it, how to use the videos, how to use the audios, and just sort of gave them a massive table of contents. And again, said like, if you're just a newbie, start here. If you're experienced in this, start here. If you're experienced here, start here. ## [12:08 - 13:11]

So I loved the book. That was, to me, this was the most important thing. And you know, this is all sort of gravy. But when I heard back from people, some people just love... Some people loved the book. They thought the books were amazing. But some people thought these videos were by far the best. Some people thought these expert interviews were amazing. And different people liked different ones. Like one of the experts was actually someone who was sort of a newbie. And one of them was someone who had been trading for 40 years. And different people liked different ones because the newbies were like, oh man, that guy had been trading for 40 years. I can't even begin to identify with him. But that newbie, man, that was awesome. I loved listening to her. So different people reacted differently to different parts of the course. So it wasn't like I just threw in all this extra stuff and just to raise the price from 50 bucks to 500. The reality is that added massive value in doing all this. But that shows you how you can go from taking a book and blowing that out into a full-out home study course.
